decisions.title_en: Anthropic agrees terms on $30B round at $900B valuation; Dragoneer/Greenoaks/Sequoia/Altimeter co-lead
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-08 (FT: Anthropic in talks for up to $50B targeting $900B-$1T). New info: terms now AGREED, round resized to $30B at $900B pre-money, four co-leads named (Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Sequoia, Altimeter, each >=$2B), three of four also hold OpenAI positions, ARR crossing $45B, could close this month.

decisions.title_en: Trump-Xi Beijing summit concludes empty-handed: no signed AI governance framework, zero H200 chips shipped, rare earths still lagging
decisions.reason: Story published across 05-12/05-13/05-14 (CEO list, Huang added to delegation, Day 1 framework + AI governance talks launched). New info: summit CONCLUDED 05-15 with NO signed bilateral AI governance document, not a single Nvidia H200 shipped to the 10 approved Chinese buyers, rare-earth exports still ~50% below pre-restriction levels; Beijing signaling pivot to domestic chips.

decisions.title_en: Amp raises $1.3B to build a shared 'AI compute grid'; founder ex-a16z Anjney Midha, Mistral/ElevenLabs/Black Forest Labs as founding members
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. $1.3B raise (a16z, YC, cloud providers) for a pooled compute grid; targets 1.9 GW over 5 years / 200 MW by end-2026; up to $500M of profits to a social-impact fund through 2030. event_date 05-12, within 5-day window.

decisions.title_en: Richard Socher's Recursive Superintelligence exits stealth: $650M at $4.65B, GV/Greycroft lead, Nvidia/AMD in, mission = automate AI R&D itself
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. London/SF lab out of stealth 05-14 with $650M at $4.65B; founders Richard Socher, Tim Rocktaschel, Jeff Clune, Josh Tobin, Tim Shi; GV+Greycroft lead, Nvidia+AMD Ventures in; thesis = automate the entire AI research pipeline (recursive self-improvement). Tier-1 founders/investors clear the significance bar (supersedes the earlier SUI Group crypto framing of the same raise).

decisions.title_en: GitLab 'Act 2' restructuring for the agentic era: flatten 3 mgmt layers, ~60 R&D teams, -30% country footprint, stock -9.6%
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback (distinct from gm-it-layoffs-ai-pivot 05-12 and cloudflare-1100-ai-layoffs 05-08 - different company, specific Act 2 plan). event_date 05-11, exactly 5 days (eligible, not >5).

decisions.title_en: Cerebras (CBRS) falls ~10% to ~$280 on second trading day
decisions.reason: Published 05-12 (upsize) and 05-14 (Day-1 +68% close $311.07). A single -10% second-day pullback (~$280, ~$95B mcap) is expected post-IPO drift and adds no substantive new fact beyond 'pop fades'.

decisions.title_en: Musk v. Altman: jury still set to deliberate Monday; no weekend court activity
decisions.reason: Closing arguments and Monday jury+remedies schedule already published 05-14. No court activity over 05-15/05-16 weekend; 05-15 coverage merely recaps the Monday schedule and possible Altman removal/unwind already reported.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic $1.5B Bartz settlement fairness hearing 'cruises'; final approval still pending
decisions.reason: Published 05-14 covering the same-day fairness hearing (91.3% claimed, $3000/work). No confirmed final-approval ruling issued on 05-15/05-16; reporting only restates that the hearing went smoothly and approval 'could come soon'.

decisions.title_en: China still hasn't approved H200 imports; zero chips shipped to approved buyers
decisions.reason: Published 05-14 (US Commerce issued export licenses for ~10 Chinese giants). The 'zero chips shipped / China pivoting domestic' development is folded into the trump-xi-china-summit-ceos-invited 05-15 update; no separate substantive H200-specific item to avoid a near-duplicate.

decisions.reason: event_date 2026-05-07 is 9 days old (>5 day limit).

decisions.reason: Within window and novel, but routine API model deprecation/redirect - below the significance bar for the briefing.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI ships new voice-intelligence models in the API (GPT-Realtime-2/Translate/Whisper)
decisions.reason: Initially shortlisted with a mistaken 05-14 date; the primary TechCrunch report URL is dated 2026-05-07, making the announcement 9 days old (>5 day limit).
